metadata.title: Sample Document for Token Chunking
metadata.author: Test Author
metadata.description: A sample extraction output for testing token-based re-chunking
subject: Testing
Embeddings

chunks.text: This is the first paragraph. It contains several sentences to test token counting. The extraction library produces word-based chunks, but we need token-based chunks for embedding model training. This paragraph should be long enough to test the chunking algorithm properly.
sentences: This is the first paragraph.
It contains several sentences to test token counting.
The extraction library produces word-based chunks, but we need token-based chunks for embedding model training.
This paragraph should be long enough to test the chunking algorithm properly.

chunks.text: The second paragraph continues with more content. We need to ensure that overlapping chunks work correctly when spanning multiple source chunks. Token-based chunking allows us to target specific token counts that are optimal for embedding models. The embeddinggemma-300m model has a 2048 token context window, which we must respect.
sentences: The second paragraph continues with more content.
We need to ensure that overlapping chunks work correctly when spanning multiple source chunks.
Token-based chunking allows us to target specific token counts that are optimal for embedding models.
The embeddinggemma-300m model has a 2048 token context window, which we must respect.

chunks.text: The third paragraph introduces new concepts. Different use cases require different chunk sizes. For retrieval tasks, smaller chunks of 256-400 tokens provide better precision. For recommendation tasks, larger chunks of 512-700 tokens preserve more context. The token-rechunk tool supports both modes through CLI presets.
sentences: The third paragraph introduces new concepts.
Different use cases require different chunk sizes.
For retrieval tasks, smaller chunks of 256-400 tokens provide better precision.
For recommendation tasks, larger chunks of 512-700 tokens preserve more context.
The token-rechunk tool supports both modes through CLI presets.

chunks.text: Sentence-aware overlap is crucial for maintaining context. A 10-20% overlap between consecutive chunks improves retrieval recall. The algorithm respects sentence boundaries to avoid splitting sentences mid-way. This preserves semantic coherence in each chunk. Validation ensures no chunk exceeds the 2048 token limit.
sentences: Sentence-aware overlap is crucial for maintaining context.
A 10-20% overlap between consecutive chunks improves retrieval recall.
The algorithm respects sentence boundaries to avoid splitting sentences mid-way.
This preserves semantic coherence in each chunk.
Validation ensures no chunk exceeds the 2048 token limit.

chunks.text: The final paragraph wraps up our test document. It demonstrates how metadata is preserved during re-chunking. Each output chunk maintains hierarchy information from the source. This allows downstream systems to understand document structure. The JSONL output format is compatible with sentence-transformers training pipelines.
sentences: The final paragraph wraps up our test document.
It demonstrates how metadata is preserved during re-chunking.
Each output chunk maintains hierarchy information from the source.
This allows downstream systems to understand document structure.
The JSONL output format is compatible with sentence-transformers training pipelines.
